title: The Pilgrimage of the Life of Man
summary: The repository artifact is a pinned, identifier-enriched copy of the 2006 University of Michigan OCR/XML encoding of Furnivall and Locock's three-part EETS edition. That edition prints a composite text from Cotton Vitellius C XIII, Cotton Tiberius A VII/1, and Stowe 952, but substitutes Chaucer's ABC through Furnivall's One-Text/CUL Ff.5.30 chain and supplements the acrostic from a Petit/Rembolt print. Vitellius and Stowe reserve blank ABC space, whereas Tiberius loses the ABC locus through missing leaves. Raw, pinned, and May 2026 current XML layers; 862 carrier images plus two scanner charts; original-part scans; the 1973 Kraus reprint; official catalog sources; and jurisdiction- and layer-specific rights determinations remain distinct.
review_notes: The 25,421 verse-line figure belongs to the descriptive work manifest; lineage preserves the sources and editorial practices needed to audit it.
The four law/guidance entities are intentional contextual isolates in the primary transmission graph and have no invented transmission, describes, or consulted relations.

entities.label: General Corpus pinned XML for ajt8111
entities.description: The exact repository XML consumed by General Corpus through the pinned CME submodule.
notes: The pinned file is 1,538,755 bytes. Its normalized text and tag sequence descend from the raw U-M XML, with repository identifier enrichment.

entities.label: The Pilgrimage of the Life of Man (Middle English work)
entities.description: A provisional conceptual work node for the one Middle English translation/adaptation commonly attributed to John Lydgate; catalog_description is used because the schema has no abstract-work entity type.
notes: This node is not a physical catalog record or digital carrier and does not collapse exact manuscript, edition, encoding, or repository layers.

entities.label: Pèlerinage de la vie humaine, second recension
entities.description: A provisional conceptual source-work node for Guillaume de Deguileville's 1355 second recension; catalog_description is used because the schema has no abstract-work entity type.
notes: The inherited XML/MARC value 1335 is a bibliographic error, not the normalized recension date.

entities.label: Furnivall–Locock EETS edition, Extra Series 77, 83, and 92
entities.description: Composite scholarly edition issued in 1899, 1901, and 1904: Furnivall's text in parts I-II and Locock's introduction, notes, glossary, and indexes in part III.

bibliographic.citation: Guillaume de Deguileville, The Pilgrimage of the Life of Man, English by John Lydgate, ed. F. J. Furnivall, with introduction, notes, glossary, and indexes by Katharine B. Locock, EETS Extra Series 77, 83, 92 (London, 1899, 1901, 1904).
bibliographic.title: The Pilgrimage of the Life of Man
bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society, Extra Series 77, 83, 92
bibliographic.publisher: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trübner & Co. for the Early English Text Society

notes: The text ends at page 665 in part II. Part III is apparatus, not a continuous third target-text segment.

entities.label: EETS Extra Series 83, text part II (1901)

entities.label: EETS Extra Series 92, apparatus part III (1904)
entities.description: Locock's introduction, notes, glossary, and indexes, containing discontinuous quotations and descriptions of the target work.

bibliographic.citation: Katharine B. Locock, introduction, notes, glossary, and indexes to The Pilgrimage of the Life of Man, EETS Extra Series 92 (1904).

entities.label: British Library, Cotton MS Vitellius C XIII
entities.description: Fifteenth-century parchment principal witness, damaged in the 1731 fire; the ABC locus preserves deliberately reserved blank space rather than a copied ABC.

notes: Damaged by the 1731 Cotton Library fire.
The reserved blank near f. 256b/257 is not a missing-leaf lacuna and does not source the EETS ABC.

entities.label: British Library, Cotton MS Tiberius A VII/1
entities.description: Second-quarter fifteenth-century parchment fragment used for Furnivall lines 18,313-23,676, with documented missing-leaf gaps.

notes: Current title uses John Lydgate (?).
Missing leaves after f. 62 remove lines 19,712-20,416, including the whole ABC locus; a second gap removes 20,557-20,812 after f. 64.
The ABC absence is a physical lacuna, not a reserved blank.

entities.label: British Library, Stowe MS 952
entities.description: Paper witness in a late-fifteenth-century hand through f. 303b and completed by John Stow around 1600.

notes: Late-fifteenth-century hand through f. 303b; remainder by John Stow.
At f. 329b Stow reserved three leaves and marked A, B, C, etc. for stanza starts but did not copy the ABC text.
This deliberate blank differs from Tiberius's missing-leaf lacuna.

entities.label: Cambridge University Library, MS Ff.5.30
entities.description: Late-fourteenth-century parchment witness of an English prose first-recension pilgrimage and carrier of the ABC used by Furnivall's One-Text edition.

holding.notes: Historical catalog places the prose pilgrimage at ff. 1a-136a and an alphabetical poem at ff. 108-111b; Furnivall cites 112v-115v.

notes: It is not a witness of Lydgate's second-recension verse translation. Preserve historical ff. 108-111b and Furnivall's 112v-115v as parallel reported locators.

entities.label: Furnivall, A One-Text Print of Chaucer's Minor Poems, volume 1 (1871)
entities.description: The source edition from which Furnivall supplied Chaucer's ABC in the Lydgate EETS text.

bibliographic.citation: Frederick J. Furnivall, ed., A One-Text Print of Chaucer's Minor Poems, vol. 1 (London, 1871).
bibliographic.title: A One-Text Print of Chaucer's Minor Poems

entities.label: Furnivall, A One-Text Print of Chaucer's Minor Poems, volume 1 (1881 reprint)
entities.description: The 1881 reprint imaged by the Bayerische Staatsbibliothek; the ABC appears on pages 85-99.

bibliographic.citation: Frederick J. Furnivall, ed., A One-Text Print of Chaucer's Minor Poems, vol. 1, 1881 reprint of the 1871 edition.

entities.label: Bayerische Staatsbibliothek IIIF facsimile bsb11664972
entities.description: Complete 114-canvas official facsimile of the 1881 One-Text reprint; the ABC is on printed pages 85-99, images 93-107.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation 2 manifest and 114 JPEG images

entities.label: Jean Petit and Berthold Rembolt, Le Romant des trois pelerinaiges (circa 1517)
entities.description: Early printed edition used through Alfred Huth's copy for the remainder of the acrostic; exact Huth-copy identity remains unverified.

bibliographic.citation: Le Romant des trois pelerinaiges (Paris: Jean Petit and Berthold Rembolt, [circa 1517]).
bibliographic.title: Le Romant des trois pelerinaiges

notes: BP16 103260 identifies two Gallica same-edition copies; neither is asserted to be Furnivall's Huth copy.

entities.label: Antoine Vérard, Le Pelerinage de lhomme (4 April 1511)
entities.description: Early printed comparator whose Latin prose tract Locock compares conditionally with the tract used by Lydgate.

bibliographic.citation: Le Pelerinage de lhomme nouvellement imprime (Paris: Antoine Vérard, 4 April 1511).

notes: Comparator only; no claim that this printed carrier was Lydgate's direct exemplar.

entities.label: Stürzinger, Le Pèlerinage de vie humaine, first-recension edition (1893)
entities.description: Johann Jakob Stürzinger's collateral first-recension edition based on BnF fr. 1818, consulted by Furnivall/Locock.

bibliographic.citation: Guillaume de Deguileville, Le Pèlerinage de vie humaine, ed. Johann Jakob Stürzinger (Roxburghe Club, 1893).

notes: Collateral scholarship on the first recension, not a source ancestor of Lydgate's second-recension work.

entities.label: Catalogue of the Manuscripts Preserved in the Library of the University of Cambridge, volume 2 (1856)
entities.description: Historical catalog source for Ff.V.30/Ff.5.30, including its prose pilgrimage and inserted alphabetical poem.

bibliographic.citation: A Catalogue of the Manuscripts Preserved in the Library of the University of Cambridge, vol. 2 (Cambridge, 1856), pp. 492-493.

rights.component: Pinned XML serialization and embedded source content
rights.basis: The embedded legacy notice says copyright applies where applicable and other distribution restrictions may apply; the repository's software-oriented top-level license is not treated as an override for source content.

rights.component: Exact Middle English work wording, including manuscript readings not demonstrably published
rights.jurisdiction: United Kingdom and other non-U.S. jurisdictions
rights.basis: Age of the manuscript carriers does not establish a worldwide status for every exact reading; U.K. and other jurisdictional publication histories were not reconstructed.

rights.component: Exact French second-recension work wording across witness traditions
rights.basis: No global publication and term analysis was completed for every exact source-work reading.

rights.component: Contributions actually published in EETS parts I-III, 1899-1904
rights.basis: The 1899-1904 published edition contributions are beyond the maximum 95-year U.S. term for applicable pre-1978 copyrights; section 104A provides only the remainder of the otherwise applicable U.S. term.
notes: This is a United States determination only and does not resolve every exact unpublished manuscript reading or a worldwide status.

rights.component: Published 1899 text-part expression
rights.basis: This part is within the scoped 1899-1904 EETS publication determination; the record does not extend to unseen witness material or non-U.S. jurisdictions.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Three non-coextensive principal witnesses with damaged, fragmentary, medieval, and later-hand portions.
editorial_practices.representation: Furnivall's composite text uses Vitellius principally, supplements it with Tiberius and Stowe, and documents source changes in notes and apparatus.
editorial_practices.description: The edition must not be modeled as a whole-to-whole transcription from each witness.
editorial_practices.scope: EETS text pages 1-665, with exact witness scopes retained in relations

editorial_practices.source_feature: Vitellius and Stowe reserve blank ABC spaces, while Tiberius loses the ABC locus through missing leaves.
editorial_practices.representation: Furnivall supplies Chaucer's ABC from his One-Text edition/CUL Ff.5.30 chain.
editorial_practices.description: Reserved copying space and a physical lacuna are distinct absence mechanisms; none of the three principal Lydgate witnesses supplies the inserted ABC text.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Stowe supplies only the initial acrostic portion.
editorial_practices.representation: Furnivall supplies the remainder from Alfred Huth's copy of the Petit/Rembolt edition.
editorial_practices.description: The exact Huth physical exemplar remains distinct from same-edition Gallica copies.

editorial_practices.source_feature: A bound three-part EETS carrier of 862 mapped book images plus two scanner charts.
editorial_practices.representation: OCR-derived XML transcribes target pages 1-665 while preserving positional PB placeholders for omitted front and back matter.
editorial_practices.description: The XML is page-map complete for 862 carrier images but not a text-complete transcription of all printed apparatus.
editorial_practices.scope: Raw XML FRONT/BODY/BACK and PB sequence 00000001-00000862

editorial_practices.source_feature: The prose tract is encoded as L print rows but contains an embedded four-stanza prayer.
editorial_practices.representation: The 727-row prose remainder and 32-line prayer are separated at the smallest defensible scope; the prayer rubric is excluded.
editorial_practices.description: This preserves printed form and intellectual verse structure instead of treating every L element as a verse line.
editorial_practices.scope: XML 3.3.7 excluding and including child 3.3.7.6 as separate scopes

evidence.citation: General Corpus pinned CME XML for ajt8111.
evidence.locator: HEADER; FRONT/BODY/BACK; all 26,346 L and 862 PB elements
evidence.summary: Confirms the exact repository artifact, identifier, source statement, text, structure, PB sequence, production declaration, and embedded legacy rights notice.

evidence.citation: Raw, pinned, and current AJT8111 digital-layer comparison and page-map audit.
evidence.locator: 869 ZIP members; raw XML, tagged text, header, pageview.dat, loadcd.log, and TIFF 00000001-00000864
evidence.summary: Verifies 864 TIFFs, 862 pageview and PB rows, scanner-chart boundary, raw/pinned normalized equivalence with enrichment, and current vocabulary normalization plus two substantive restorations.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive raw U-M AJT8111 Michigan Book package and item metadata.
evidence.summary: Verifies package identity, fixity, timestamps, 869 members, raw XML and tagged-text members, 864 TIFF MD5 values, 862/2 carrier-versus-QA boundary, and absence of IA rights/license metadata.

evidence.citation: Current U-M/medictionary AJT8111 XML and May 2026 CME download sources.
evidence.summary: Verifies the current normalized XML, exact fixity, public-domain/free-of-known-restrictions header assertion, and the current no-restrictions-on-use-or-reuse download statement.

evidence.citation: Furnivall–Locock EETS edition, Extra Series 77, 83, and 92 (1899, 1901, 1904).
evidence.locator: cumulative title; text pp. 1-665; part III introduction and notes
evidence.summary: Establishes the three-part publication sequence, Furnivall and Locock roles, text boundary, 1426 beginning, 1355 second recension, witness use, embedded-source substitutions, and apparatus scope.

evidence.citation: Original EETS Extra Series 77 part-I scan and Internet Archive metadata.
evidence.summary: Verifies the 1899 part, printed pages 1-240, PDF images 19-258, and target edition lines 1-8,684.

evidence.citation: Original EETS Extra Series 83 part-II scan and Internet Archive metadata.
evidence.summary: Verifies the 1901 part, printed pages 241-665, PDF images 11-435, target edition lines 8,685-24,832, and printed prose layout at pages 437-456.

evidence.citation: Original EETS Extra Series 92 part-III scan and Internet Archive metadata.
evidence.locator: introduction pp. lxvii*-lxviii*; notes and glossary
evidence.summary: Verifies Locock's 1904 apparatus, manuscript descriptions, Tiberius missing leaves, prose-tract analysis, comparator citations, and discontinuous quoted target material.

evidence.citation: University of California Internet Archive scan of the combined EETS edition.
evidence.summary: Direct visual inspection establishes an unaltered 1973 Kraus one-volume reprint, not 1978; the complete PDF contains target text and apparatus.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive U-M/Google scan of a bound original EETS set.
evidence.summary: Verifies a complete bound original three-part carrier distinct from the 1973 Kraus reprint.

evidence.citation: British Library parent and component records for Cotton MS Vitellius C XIII.
evidence.summary: Establish witness identity, target component ff. 2-311, fifteenth-century parchment support, fire damage, and current translator attribution.

evidence.citation: Biblissima record for Cotton MS Vitellius C XIII.
evidence.summary: Supplies an independent manuscript/work description and a four-line target incipit serialized in the cached metadata.

evidence.citation: British Library parent and component records for Cotton MS Tiberius A VII/1.
evidence.summary: Establish fragment identity, ff. 39-106, second-quarter fifteenth-century parchment support, questioned Lydgate title, edition-line range, and unavailable digital images.

evidence.citation: Biblissima record for Cotton MS Tiberius A VII.
evidence.summary: Supplies an independent fragment/work description, fols. 39r-106v, and a four-line target incipit serialized in the cached metadata.

evidence.citation: British Library catalog record for Stowe MS 952.
evidence.summary: Establishes the paper witness, late-fifteenth-century and John Stow hands, common Lydgate attribution, quoted target passages, and three reserved ABC leaves.

evidence.citation: Current British Library and historical/edition witness descriptions for AJT8111.
evidence.summary: Synthesizes the three principal Lydgate witnesses, exact surviving scopes, Stow completion, Vitellius/Stowe reserved ABC blanks, and Tiberius missing-leaf lacuna without asserting public manuscript images.

evidence.citation: British Library, Digitisation Services and Imaging Services terms and conditions.
evidence.locator: Online ordering; required shelfmark/title/folio information; manuscript photography limits; fulfillment, pricing, payment, and copyright conditions
evidence.summary: Confirms a current mediated order route for images from British Library manuscripts. Orders require exact shelfmark and folio details and remain conditional on retrieval, condition, security, rights, payment, and item-specific review; the service page does not establish that AJT8111 witness images already exist or can be supplied.
notes: The terms page states it was updated in January 2026. No form, rate sheet, or terms file was downloaded into the research cache.

evidence.citation: 1856 Cambridge manuscript catalogue, volume 2, and Internet Archive scan/OCR.
evidence.summary: Describes Ff.V.30/Ff.5.30 as a 160-leaf late-fourteenth-century parchment quarto, its prose first-recension pilgrimage, and alphabetical poem at historical ff. 108-111b.

evidence.citation: Cambridge University Library, Image Supply and Reproduction, with current terms and online request route.
evidence.locator: Ordering images; online form; quoting, payment, delivery, special-collections review, and permission-to-use terms
evidence.summary: Confirms a current mediated CUL imaging and permissions route. Special-collections requests may require curator or conservator review before a quote, supplied images and further use are separately conditioned, and the route does not establish that Ff.5.30 images already exist or that the manuscript can be imaged.
notes: The current terms page says it was reviewed in May 2025. No form, rate sheet, or terms file was downloaded into the research cache.

evidence.citation: Bayerische Staatsbibliothek IIIF object bsb11664972 for the 1881 One-Text reprint.
evidence.summary: Verifies 114 canvases, ABC pages 85-99/images 93-107, Furnivall's Ff.5.30 leaf references, and the NoC-NC 1.0 provider statement.

evidence.citation: BP16 record 103260 for Le Romant des trois pelerinaiges.
evidence.summary: Establishes Paris, Jean Petit and Berthold Rembolt, circa 1517, same-edition Gallica ARKs, and Morgan among holding institutions.

evidence.citation: BP16 record 101729 for Le Pelerinage de lhomme.
evidence.summary: Establishes Paris, Antoine Vérard, 4 April 1511, and exact same-edition Gallica ARKs.

evidence.citation: Stürzinger 1893 first-recension edition, complete Internet Archive PDF and metadata.
evidence.summary: Verifies the collateral edition, complete 532-page carrier, and Internet Archive Public Domain Mark metadata.

evidence.citation: EETS, One-Text/CUL, BP16, and Stürzinger evidence for embedded and comparative source changes.
evidence.summary: Supports the supplied ABC chain, parallel CUL foliation reports, Stowe/Petit acrostic split, Vérard prose comparison, Stürzinger collateral use, and exact-exemplar caveats.

evidence.citation: U.S. Copyright Office, Circular 15A, Duration of Copyright, revised April 2026.
evidence.locator: pp. 1-2 and revision line; maximum 95-year term and current pre-1931 statement
evidence.summary: Supports the current pre-1931/publication-term analysis for the scoped U.S. EETS-edition determination.

evidence.citation: Official U.S. Copyright Office HTML delivery of 17 U.S.C. chapter 1, including section 104A.
evidence.summary: Supports the rule that a restored work receives only the remainder of the otherwise applicable U.S. term.

evidence.citation: Official U.S. Copyright Office HTML delivery of 17 U.S.C. chapter 3, including section 304.
evidence.summary: Supports the maximum 95-year term for applicable subsisting pre-1978 copyrights.

evidence.citation: U.K. Intellectual Property Office, Copyright Notice: Duration of copyright.
evidence.summary: Documents the pre-1989 unpublished-work 2039 rule as a reason not to assert blanket U.K. status; it does not prove that a particular AJT8111 reading was unpublished at the end of 1988.

open_questions.question: Can Alfred Huth's exact copy of the Petit/Rembolt circa-1517 edition be verified as Morgan Library PML 62412?
next_steps: Obtain a current authoritative Morgan record or physical-copy evidence before minting a Huth/Morgan copy entity.
Do not substitute a same-edition Gallica copy for Furnivall's exact exemplar.

open_questions.question: Which one best complete official Gallica carrier can be successfully acquired for each of the Petit/Rembolt and Vérard editions?
next_steps: Retry one exact official ARK per edition from a working network path.
Avoid duplicate same-edition acquisition unless copies provide distinct evidence.

open_questions.question: Can complete public target facsimiles be verified for Vitellius C XIII, Tiberius A VII/1, or Stowe 952?
next_steps: Check for a newly public complete facsimile separately from the mediated order routes.
If authorized and funded, submit item-specific reproduction enquiries without treating requestability as evidence that images already exist or can be supplied.

open_questions.question: How should the exact U-M physical bound EETS set scanned in 2003 be modeled if the schema gains a physical-print-copy entity type?
next_steps: Keep physical-copy identity in capture notes until the schema supports a distinct printed-carrier entity.

open_questions.question: When was the full Middle English translation completed?
next_steps: Consult manuscript, documentary, and recent Lydgate scholarship; retain begun 1426 until completion evidence is found.

open_questions.question: How should historical CUL ff. 108-111b and Furnivall's 112v-115v ABC locators be reconciled?
next_steps: Inspect Ff.5.30 directly or obtain current authoritative foliation documentation.
Preserve both reported locator layers in the meantime.

open_questions.question: Can the EETS text's witness switching be reconstructed line by line beyond the explicit assignments modeled here?
next_steps: Audit Furnivall's sigla and notes across both text parts before narrowing relation scopes further.

open_questions.question: What is the U.K. and other non-U.S. status of exact manuscript readings not shown to have been published, and of Locock's apparatus outside the United States?
next_steps: Reconstruct first-publication and author-death facts by exact component and jurisdiction.
Do not generalize the scoped U.S. edition conclusion to unpublished witness readings or worldwide status.
notes: The U.K. guidance is risk/context evidence only; it does not prove that a particular reading was unpublished at the end of 1988.
